commit.message:
fix: run all Dashboard API calls in single allSettled batch with 5s timeout

Previous approach had 3 sequential batches x 10s timeout = 30s total,
which matched the test timeout exactly. Now all 8 API calls run in a
single Promise.allSettled batch with 5s timeouts, guaranteeing loadAll
completes within ~5s even if all calls hang.

commit.message:
fix: poll Ynison state during pause instead of blocking on event

Same-track resume doesn't trigger _track_changed_event (only track
change and seek do). Poll every 1s with a 30s deadline so the stream
unblocks immediately when Ynison reports is_paused=False.

commit.message:
fix: upgrade auth failure logging from debug to warn

JWT validation failures and auth rejections were only logged at debug level,
making it impossible to see WHY Bearer tokens were rejected in production logs.

Changes:
- JWT validation failure: debug → warn with error message
- Userinfo fallback failure: debug → warn with error message
- Auth middleware 401: now logs route, method, and whether a Bearer token was present

This helps diagnose GT3 app 401 errors (expired tokens during background reconnect).

commit.message:
Add clipboard copy/paste support (l_clipboard_set/get)

Windows: Uses Win32 OpenClipboard/SetClipboardData/GetClipboardData (CF_TEXT).
X11: Full selection protocol — InternAtom for CLIPBOARD/UTF8_STRING/TARGETS,
  SetSelectionOwner to claim, SelectionRequest/SelectionNotify event handling
  for cross-app paste, ConvertSelection+GetProperty for paste-from-others.
Framebuffer/WASI: Internal static buffer (within-process clipboard).

New API in l_gfx.h:
  l_clipboard_set(canvas, text, len) — copy text to clipboard
  l_clipboard_get(canvas, buf, max) — paste from clipboard

Tests: test_clipboard.c (10 assertions — roundtrip, overwrite, truncation)
README: Updated header table and function reference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: laststanding
repository.description: Experiments with freestanding c

commit.message:
Fix map canvas clipping: remove explicit height, use min()+aspect-ratio

Having both width:420px and height:420px meant when max-width:100%
shrank the width, height stayed 420px — making the canvas taller than
its container so the top got clipped by overflow:hidden.

Now: width:min(420px,100%) lets it fill the panel up to 420px,
and aspect-ratio:1/1 derives height from width — always square,
never overflowing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent service kill during AA disconnect cycles

Three-pronged fix for service being killed repeatedly during driving:

1. Override onUpdateNotification to keep foreground service alive whenever
   there's a queue, even when paused. This prevents Android from killing
   the background service after Media3 removes the notification.

2. When AA disconnects and triggers a pause, set pausedByAutoDisconnect
   flag so onPlayWhenReadyChanged does NOT abandon audio focus. This
   keeps the notification posted and the service in foreground.

3. Add scheduleForegroundTimeout (10 min) so the service eventually
   releases resources if the user doesn't resume after an extended pause.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add crash types 19-26, fix post-build analysis accuracy

Crash types added:
- Type 19: RESOURCE_SHRUNK_BY_NAME — getIdentifier() with strict shrinkResources
- Type 20: GENERIC_SIGNATURE_STRIPPED — TypeToken generic type erasure
- Type 21: COMPONENT_CLASS_NOT_FOUND — ComponentName/setClassName string reference
- Type 22: DATA_CLASS_MEMBER_STRIPPED — copy()/componentN() removed by R8 full mode
- Type 23: DYNAMIC_PROXY_STRIPPED — Proxy.newProxyInstance() interface renamed
- Type 24: JSON_ASSET_RESOURCE_STRIPPED — resources referenced only in JSON assets
- Type 25: FRAGMENT_CLASS_RENAMED — FragmentManager saved state FQCN mismatch
- Type 26: SERIALIZABLE_CLASS_RENAMED — cross-build deserialization failure

Plugin improvements:
- Add assetsDirs property to PostBuildAnalysisTask for JSON asset scanning
- Fix SerializableAnalyzer to handle multi-line data class declarations
- Fix KOTLIN_OBJECT_INSTANCE_REMOVED: use renamed-field check not absence check
  (mapping.txt only lists renamed entries; absence means preserved, not removed)
- Fix DEVIRTUALIZATION_ILLEGAL_ACCESS: note whether class was renamed by keep rule
- Improve JAVASCRIPT_INTERFACE and NATIVE_METHOD fix rules to keep class itself
- Add fix rules for types 7, 14, 15, 18 (DataProcessor, WebBridgeHandler, etc.)
- Add HtmlReportGenerator exhaustive when branches for types 24-26

Verification results:
- Crash mode (no keep rules): 29 confirmed errors
- Fix mode (all keep rules applied): 1 remaining (devirtualization — inherent
  code issue, not preventable by ProGuard keep rules alone)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add timeout to Dashboard API calls to prevent hanging

- Dashboard loadAll() now uses Promise.allSettled with 10s per-call timeout
- Prevents the entire page from hanging if any single API call stalls

test(spec): cover Deserialize error path when input is not a string

The `Deserialize for Spec` impl (spec.rs:293) calls
`String::deserialize(deserializer)?`.  Previously every call went through
the happy path (a JSON string) so the `?`-operator error branch — taken
when the JSON token is a number, bool, or object — was never exercised.

The new test `spec_deserialize_non_string_returns_error` passes the raw
JSON number `42` to `serde_json::from_str::<Spec>`, causing
`String::deserialize` to fail and the `?` error branch to be taken for
the first time.

Coverage delta (libaipm/src/spec.rs):
  - Regions missed:  17 → 16  (-1)
  - Lines missed:     4 →  4  (no change in line count, but region covered)
  - Branches missed: 13 → 13  (unchanged; LLVM counts `?` as region, not branch)
Overall TOTAL:
  - Missed regions:  2350 → 2348  (-2)
  - Missed lines:    1238 → 1237  (-1)
  - Branch coverage: 95.13%       (unchanged, already above 89% gate)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fixes #4932 - Enable `Inline` apps in addition to `Full Screen` (#4933)

* Initial plan

* Add AppModel enum, Application.AppModel config property, AnsiOutput inline mode, OutputBufferImpl.ClearContents(bool), InlineCLI example

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/9087ee40-33d2-4446-9d01-60df80dba4a3

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Set Screen from first layout pass in inline mode; update InlineCLI example to use Pos.AnchorEnd() and Dim.Auto(minimumContentDim: 10)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/7427eedb-1394-405b-a255-533e5a5fdea9

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reserve vertical space in inline mode to handle cursor-at-bottom; position cursor below on dispose

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/03480b9f-9dd3-4a5d-acb0-a934f230e11a

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use EscSeqUtils.CSI constant instead of hardcoded escape literal

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/03480b9f-9dd3-4a5d-acb0-a934f230e11a

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* staged

* Fix inline mode double-draw by deferring initial LayoutAndDraw

Begin() was calling LayoutAndDraw() directly before the main loop started,
bypassing the IterationImpl deferral logic. In inline mode this caused a
first draw using the default 80x25 fallback size (placing the view at the
wrong row), followed by a second correct draw once the ANSI size monitor
reported the real terminal dimensions.

Changes:
- Skip LayoutAndDraw() in Begin() when AppModel.Inline is active; the
  first render is deferred to IterationImpl until InitialSizeReceived
  is confirmed (or 500ms timeout).
- Add InitialSizeReceived to ISizeMonitor/AnsiSizeMonitor so the main
  loop knows when the real terminal size has arrived.
- Add inline size deferral logic in IterationImpl with Trace
  instrumentation for diagnostics.
- Remove QueryNativeTerminalSize() from AnsiOutput (pure ANSI only).
- Update InlineCLI example: replace Label with ListView + ObservableCollection.
- Add InlineDrawTimingTests (4 trace-based tests proving deferral works).
- Add AnsiSizeMonitor InitialSizeReceived tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Position inline view at cursor row via ANSI CPR query

In inline mode, the view was always drawn at the bottom of the terminal
because AnchorEnd() positioned against the full terminal height. When the
cursor was near the top, the view appeared far below the expected position.

Fix: query the cursor position via ANSI DECXCPR (CSI ?6n) at startup and
use the response to:
- Resize Screen.Height to (termHeight - cursorRow) so the view occupies
  only the rows available below the cursor.
- Set InlineRowOffset on AnsiOutput so Screen row 0 maps to the cursor's
  terminal row in SetCursorPositionImpl.

Changes:
- ISizeMonitor: add InitialCursorRow property (default 0)
- AnsiSizeMonitor: send CPR query in Initialize() for inline mode; parse
  DECXCPR response; InitialSizeReceived now requires both size AND cursor
  position responses before returning true
- AnsiOutput: add InlineRowOffset property; apply in SetCursorPositionImpl
- ApplicationImpl.Screen: LayoutAndDraw resizes Screen and sets row offset
  on first inline draw
- ApplicationImpl: add InlineCursorRow internal property
- IterationImpl: pass cursor row from SizeMonitor when inline confirmed
- InlineCLI example: remove AnchorEnd(), view starts at Y=0
- Add 3 new tests for cursor position query behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

* Add generic ANSI startup readiness gate and integrations

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/80eb1d41-0dcf-4d44-8d13-f5d2a11ca9f4

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Tighten startup gate integration for test/runtime compatibility

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/80eb1d41-0dcf-4d44-8d13-f5d2a11ca9f4

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor inline-mode state with new InlineState struct

Replaces scattered inline-mode state with a centralized InlineState struct, updating ApplicationImpl, AnsiOutput, DriverImpl, and IDriver to use it. All inline rendering, scrolling, and layout logic now references InlineState, supporting dynamic region growth. Updates MainLoopCoordinator and ApplicationMainLoop to wire up InlineState, and enhances the InlinePromptView demo to display live state. Includes minor UI and code style improvements.

* new plan

* Address review feedback for ANSI startup gate API and optional behavior

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/23649430-6698-4d4d-a8e1-4a98ba7a047e

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix formatting issue in AnsiStartupGate

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/23649430-6698-4d4d-a8e1-4a98ba7a047e

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use startup gate completion handles in ANSI startup probes

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/da3168d4-3c90-492b-9b6b-34c304559ceb

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use RegisterQuery completion handles for startup gate probes

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/da3168d4-3c90-492b-9b6b-34c304559ceb

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor inline mode to use App.Screen sub-rectangle

Inline mode now uses App.Screen to represent the inline region as a sub-rectangle of the terminal, with Y as the row offset and Height as the region height. Output buffer and driver logic are updated to render within this region. AnsiOutput now uses App.Screen for cursor positioning and cleanup, replacing InlineState fields. Terminal resizing, scrolling, and buffer management in inline mode are clarified and simplified. Tests are updated to allow non-zero origins in fullscreen mode. Includes code style and formatting improvements.

* Shift startup gate ownership to IApplication.AnsiStartupGate

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gui-cs/Terminal.Gui/sessions/b371f236-5afe-422b-80ac-b107bf8c1ce9

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* No changes detected

No code changes were made in this commit.

* Refactor AppModel to instance property; logo as reusable view

Refactored AppModel and ForceInlineCursorRow to be instance-based properties on IApplication, IDriver, and IComponentFactory, improving testability and multi-app support. Updated all inline mode logic and tests to use instance properties. Moved logo rendering to a reusable Logo view in UICatalog, replacing GradientArtView. Improved InlinePromptView layout and output sizing. Cleaned up obsolete code and updated project references.

* Remove DEBUG-only lifecycle trace test

Removed the SizeChange_EmitsLifecycleTraces test from AnsiSizeMonitorTests. This test, which validated trace entries for size changes with ListBackend and TraceCategory.Lifecycle, was only included in DEBUG builds. No other changes were made to the test class.

* Improve inline mode: fixes, refactoring, and UI polish

Refactor InlineCLI for clarity and style, ensuring output list auto-scrolls and input clears after submission. Move version string out of Logo art and display as a separate View. Fix ApplicationImpl.Screen to force redraw after terminal scroll. Adjust mouse coordinates in inline mode for correct alignment. Apply code style improvements throughout per project conventions.

* Add InlineColorPicker example and fix inline mouse offset

Adds InlineColorPicker project demonstrating inline ColorPicker usage with new RunnableWrapper<TView, TResult> for dialog-less, typed result extraction. Fixes ApplicationMouse inline coordinate adjustment to prevent double-subtraction on synthesized events. Includes new test for mouse offset correctness and minor ColorPicker code/style improvements. Updates solution file.

* Fix AnsiSizeMonitor startup race; update inline plan doc

Fixes two race conditions in AnsiSizeMonitor: always completes the startup size query even on empty response, and ensures cursor position query completion occurs after parsing the initial row. Updates inline-mode-plan.md with detailed architecture phases and documents these fixes.

* Improve inline mode resize: reset, clear, and redraw

Updated inline mode resize logic to reset the region to row 0, clear the entire terminal, and mark the screen for re-sizing from scratch. This ensures a full redraw and correct layout after terminal resizes by resetting InlineCursorRow, sending a terminal clear sequence, and updating the Screen rectangle.

* Add tests for inline terminal resize behavior

Added InlineResizeTests to verify that after a terminal resize in inline mode, the inline region resets its Y position and width, and the driver's InlineCursorRow is reset to 0. Both tests use the ANSI driver and simulate inline mode scenarios.

* Fix inline mode overflow and add comprehensive tests

Updated ApplicationImpl.Screen.cs to prevent cursor row underflow during overflow adjustment using Math.Max. Added InlineModeTests covering App.Screen positioning, sizing, overflow handling, dynamic growth, resize behavior, output buffer state, mouse event adjustment, and App.Screen vs Driver.Screen independence. All tests follow project conventions and are marked as Copilot-generated.

* Refactor inline mode to use Point InlinePosition

Replaces InlineCursorRow/InlineState with Point InlinePosition (Y=row, X reserved). Updates all APIs, properties, and tests to use the new model. Removes InlineState struct. Improves flexibility and clarifies inline region start for future enhancements.

* Add docs for Inline Mode and usage in Terminal.Gui

Comprehensive documentation for the new Inline Mode (`AppModel.Inline`) in Terminal.Gui, including rendering modes, usage examples, coordinate system, startup sequence, dynamic growth, resize handling, output buffer, clean exit, testing strategies, and the `RunnableWrapper<TView>` utility. Suitable for CLI tools that require output in the terminal scrollback buffer.

* Update Terminal.Gui/App/IApplication.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add InlineSelect example and document ANSI inline mode

Added the InlineSelect example project demonstrating inline OptionSelector usage with command-line options and orientation flags. Updated drivers.md to document new ANSI driver inline mode features, including AppModel, InlinePosition, buffer handling, and startup/shutdown behavior. Configured solution and project files accordingly.

* Change AnsiStartupGate type in IApplication interface

The AnsiStartupGate property in IApplication was updated to use the concrete AnsiStartupGate? type instead of the IAnsiStartupGate? interface. This change affects how the property is referenced and assigned throughout the codebase.

* Restore mouse coords before popover event recursion

Restores the original terminal-absolute mouse coordinates to mouseEvent.ScreenPosition before recursively calling RaiseMouseEvent. This ensures correct offset subtraction and accurate event handling below the popover after dismissal.

* Fix inline mode scrolling and mouse offset issues

Refines inline mode to prevent extra scrolling when the view fits exactly at the terminal's bottom row and ensures the shell prompt appears correctly after inline content. Adjusts mouse event handling to avoid double subtraction of inline offsets when dismissing popovers. Adds targeted tests for both cursor positioning and mouse coordinate adjustment. Also sets OptionSelector border style to Rounded.

* Flush Windows console input buffer on exit

Add WindowsVTInputHelper.Flush() using FlushConsoleInputBuffer to clear pending input events, preventing protocol-specific sequences from leaking into the shell. Update AnsiInput to call this method for WindowsVT, ensuring consistent input buffer flushing across platforms.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Tig <tig@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Web Audio drone sound engine to exam-simulator-mr

Physical synthesis model for DJI Phantom-style quadcopter:
- 4 motor oscillator banks (sawtooth + square + sine harmonics)
  each slightly detuned (±6–14 cents) to produce inter-motor beating
- Blade-pass frequency scales with motorRpm (38 Hz idle → 275 Hz hover)
- Throttle stick adds up to +22 Hz pitch shift when climbing
- Bandpass white-noise wind layer rises with horizontal flight speed
- DynamicsCompressor prevents clipping at full motor gain

UI:
- 🔊/🔇 toggle button (#audio-toggle) beside #hud-toggle in demo mode
- Stacks to left:8px top:92px in manual mode (below 📋 📊)
- AudioContext unlocked on first click/keydown/touchstart (browser policy)
- curThrInput global captures throttle each frame for audio pitch tracking

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: replace inline k* URL constants with AppConfig named endpoints

Replace all usages of kApiBaseUrl, kApi2BaseUrl, kApi3BaseUrl,
kApi9BaseUrl, kApi19BaseUrl, kRequestBaseUrl, kPaymentBaseUrl,
and kSocketServerBaseUrl across 63 Dart files with the corresponding
AppConfig.* named constants.

- Endpoint URLs → AppConfig.signIn, AppConfig.match, etc.
- Image URL prefixes → AppConfig.imageBase
- Local _baseUrl / baseUrl fields set to kApiBaseUrl/Api2 → AppConfig.imageBase
- Socket URL → AppConfig.socketUrl
- Payment URLs → AppConfig.khaltiPayment / AppConfig.hblPayment
- Query-param URLs keep their parameters, only the base+path is swapped

No new imports added; existing app_endpoints.dart re-export makes
AppConfig available wherever the old k* constants were used.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(jetson): remove Docker artifacts, fix TRT tensor detection, sync docs

  instead of assuming index order (prevents silent wrong predictions)

  systemd ReadWritePaths includes /dev/dri for GPU access

  update Docker section from DEPRECATED to REMOVED

Disable notification interactions while action is pending

Add .notification-pending CSS class that fades the entire card
(opacity 0.45) and blocks all pointer events until the backend
responds. Simplifies handleAction to toggle the class on the
whole notification element instead of manually disabling
individual buttons/inputs.

commit.message:
fix: PNG watermark size now matches between preview and exported frames

The PNG watermark was displayed using fixed pixel caps in the preview
(WatermarkOverlay.vue) but FFmpeg overlaid the PNG at its natural/original
size, causing a large mismatch.

Fix: define watermark size as a percentage of the video width, applied
consistently in both places.

Frontend:
- WatermarkOverlay: image now uses width='X%' of the video container
  instead of fixed scaled pixels — matches FFmpeg output 1:1
- ImageOptionsPanel: added slider (1–80%) for PNG watermark size
- useImageOptions: added watermarkImageSize ref (default 15%)
  included in toProcessingOptions() as imageSize field

Backend:
- WatermarkOptions: added image_size: Option<u32> (percentage, default 15)
- ffmpeg.rs: switched from plain overlay to scale2ref filter chain so the
  PNG is scaled to iw*frac relative to the processed video width before
  overlay; works for both frames and animated GIFs
- New test: build_video_filters_image_watermark_custom_size (25% → iw*0.2500)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
